skill.name: dx-optimizer
skill.description: Developer Experience specialist. Improves tooling, setup, and workflows. Use PROACTIVELY when setting up new projects, after team feedback, or when development friction is noticed.
skill.summary: Developer Experience specialist. Improves tooling, setup, and workflows. Use PROACTIVELY when settin...
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged shell execution and weak cryptography patterns, but the cited lines are prose in SKILL.md rather than executable code. The skill is publishable with low risk because it may guide an agent to create or modify project scripts, git hooks, and task runner files.

low_findings.title: Script and Hook Modification Guidance
low_findings.description: The skill recommends improved package.json scripts and task runner setup. This is legitimate DX work, but generated commands should be reviewed before use.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines describe expected deliverables that can affect local development commands. No malicious command, network call, or secret access is present.

low_findings.title: Static Command Pattern Dismissed
low_findings.description: The referenced resource instruction and .claude command deliverable are documentation items. They do not execute shell commands by themselves.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited text asks the agent to open a markdown resource and lists a directory for command files. There is no backtick shell execution or dynamic command string.

low_findings.title: Static Weak Cryptography Pattern Dismissed
low_findings.description: The flagged lines contain ordinary descriptive words and documentation guidance. No cryptographic algorithm, hashing API, or encryption behavior appears there.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of the exact lines shows plain English text only. I found no evidence of MD5, SHA-1, DES, or other weak cryptographic use.

content.value_statement: Teams lose time when setup, tooling, and feedback loops are slow or unclear. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code identify friction and propose practical DX improvements.
actual_capabilities: Reviews developer setup and workflow friction points.
Suggests improvements for scripts, task runners, and git hooks.
Plans onboarding changes that reduce clone-to-run time.
Recommends IDE configuration and useful development tooling.
Defines metrics for build speed, test speed, and developer satisfaction.
limitations: Does not include the referenced implementation playbook file.
Does not execute benchmarks or validate commands automatically.
Needs project-specific context before recommending safe script changes.
Generated hooks and scripts require human review before adoption.

use_cases.title: Speed Up Project Onboarding
use_cases.description: Audit setup steps and create a shorter path from repository clone to running app.

use_cases.title: Improve Local Developer Tooling
use_cases.description: Identify missing scripts, aliases, hooks, and IDE settings that reduce repeated manual work.

use_cases.title: Reduce Feedback Loop Time
use_cases.description: Review build, test, and hot reload workflows to find practical speed improvements.

prompt_templates.title: Find Setup Friction
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner audit for a new repository.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this project for developer setup friction. List the slowest or least clear steps, then suggest simple improvements.

prompt_templates.title: Improve Scripts
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze the current development scripts and task runner options. Recommend safer, clearer commands for common local workflows.

prompt_templates.title: Design Onboarding Flow
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a developer onboarding plan that gets a new contributor from clone to running app in under five minutes.

prompt_templates.title: Build a DX Optimization Roadmap
prompt_templates.prompt: Assess developer workflows across setup, testing, builds, IDE configuration, and documentation. Prioritize improvements by impact, risk, and effort.

output_examples.input: Review our setup guide and package scripts for onboarding issues.
output_examples.output: A ranked list of setup blockers, missing prerequisites, unclear commands, and recommended script changes.

output_examples.input: Help reduce test feedback time for local development.
output_examples.output: A workflow assessment with timing questions, likely bottlenecks, quick wins, and metrics to track after changes.

output_examples.input: Suggest git hooks and IDE defaults for this team.
output_examples.output: A concise proposal for pre-commit checks, editor settings, and documentation updates with review notes.

best_practices: Review generated scripts and hooks before adding them to a repository.
Measure baseline setup, build, and test times before changing workflows.
Keep onboarding instructions short, current, and tested on a clean machine.
anti_patterns: Adding automation without explaining how to troubleshoot failures.
Optimizing rare workflows while common daily tasks remain slow.
Changing team scripts without confirming platform and package manager assumptions.

faq.question: Does this skill run commands automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance and proposed changes. The user or agent must decide what to run.

faq.question: Can it improve package.json scripts?
faq.answer: Yes. It can recommend clearer and more useful scripts after reviewing the project context.

faq.question: Is it only for JavaScript projects?
faq.answer: No. The guidance applies to many project types, but examples may focus on common web workflows.

faq.question: Can it create git hooks?
faq.answer: It can propose git hook configuration, but teams should review hook behavior before adoption.

faq.question: What information should I provide?
faq.answer: Provide setup steps, existing scripts, build commands, test commands, and known developer pain points.

faq.question: Does it include a playbook resource?
faq.answer: No. The skill references a playbook file, but that file is not present in this package.
